payload.answer: Rejected. Creating 500 memory cards from individual CSV rows is inappropriate durable-memory behavior. Row-level table contents are typically better handled as source data, summaries, or on-demand retrieval rather than committed as hundreds of standalone memories.
citations: Input: "LLM proposes 500 memory cards, one per CSV row"

entity_resolution.reason: No stable entity or durable personal fact should be resolved from a bulk proposal that atomizes a CSV into 500 row-level memories.

details: Blocked excessive atomization of tabular data into durable memory cards.
Recommended summarization or artifact/reference storage instead of per-row ingestion.

repair_options: Do not store one memory card per CSV row.
Store the CSV as an external artifact/reference instead of durable memory.
Create a small number of summary memories only if they capture stable, high-value, user-relevant facts.
If specific rows contain enduring personal facts, extract only those few rows and preserve uncertainty/context.
Ask for import mode selection: reference-only, summary, or targeted extraction.
